skill.name: amap-jsapi-skill
skill.description: Guides AI assistants in building web maps with AMap JSAPI v2.0, including map setup, overlays, controls, geocoding, routing, search, layers, and security configuration.
skill.summary: Build AMap JSAPI v2.0 web maps with focused API references and usage patterns.
skill.author: AMap-Web

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many hits, but most are Markdown API references and false positives from backticks, method names, and documentation terms. One material issue is confirmed: SKILL.md requires a shell curl request to an AMap logging endpoint before code generation, which creates outbound telemetry and command execution behavior. No evidence found of prompt injection or confirmed credential exfiltration, but publication should wait for removal or opt-in treatment of telemetry and safer HTML guidance.

high_findings.title: Mandatory Telemetry via Shell Command
high_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs agents to send a curl request to an AMap logging endpoint before generating code. This is a true positive for both network access and external command execution because it creates nonessential outbound telemetry from the user environment.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command template and destination URL are explicit, and the surrounding text marks the action as mandatory. The request appears telemetry-focused rather than credential exfiltration, so this is high risk but not a confirmed malicious block.

medium_findings.title: Frontend Secret Handling Requires Care
medium_findings.description: The skill requires AMAP_JSAPI_KEY metadata and shows browser-side securityJsCode configuration for development. The documentation also recommends a proxy for production, so this is legitimate AMap guidance but still easy to misuse.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment key requirement and securityJsCode examples are real, but the reference explicitly warns against production exposure. Risk depends on whether generated applications follow the proxy guidance.

medium_findings.title: Unsafe HTML Pattern in InfoWindow Examples
medium_findings.description: The references use innerHTML to build custom info window content. The shown content is static, but generated variants could become XSS-prone if user-controlled text is inserted without sanitization.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The innerHTML assignments are directly present, but the examples do not show untrusted input. This is a moderate documentation risk because assistants may adapt the pattern unsafely.

low_findings.title: Large Static Finding Set Is Mostly Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Most Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, Windows SAM, system reconnaissance, and obfuscation hits occur inside Markdown references or API names. Representative lines show code fences, map API method names, and ordinary documentation text rather than executable malicious code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of representative flagged lines shows Markdown examples and API reference terms, not runnable hidden code. The remaining risk is the real telemetry instruction and unsafe examples captured separately.

dangerous_patterns.title: curl Command to External Logging Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains a bash curl command template that sends a GET request to restapi.amap.com for skill usage logging.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell command and URL are explicit in the skill instructions, and the text says to run it before code generation.

dangerous_patterns.title: HTML Injection-Prone API Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The info window reference demonstrates assigning template HTML with innerHTML, which can become dangerous if future generated code interpolates untrusted data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The assignments are present, but the sampled content is static. The risk is in reuse of this pattern with dynamic content.

content.value_statement: Building AMap web maps requires many small API choices across loaders, controls, overlays, and services. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured references for producing JSAPI v2.0 map implementations.
actual_capabilities: Explains AMapLoader setup for JSAPI v2.0 and common plugin loading patterns.
Provides reference guidance for map lifecycle, view control, zoom, center, pitch, and rotation.
Covers markers, label markers, vector graphics, custom info windows, context menus, and overlay groups.
Documents common AMap services such as geocoder, route planning, place search, weather, and geolocation.
Includes guidance for official layers, WMS, WMTS, canvas layers, custom layers, and 3D map views.
Highlights security key handling and recommends proxy-based production configuration.
limitations: The skill is documentation only and does not provide a runnable test harness.
Generated map examples still need valid AMap keys and service permissions.
Some examples use placeholder Chinese text and must be localized for the target application.
Security-sensitive examples need review before production use, especially HTML content and key handling.

use_cases.title: Prototype a map page
use_cases.description: Create an AMap JSAPI page with loader setup, a 3D map, controls, and a safe teardown flow.

use_cases.title: Add location services
use_cases.description: Use geocoding, POI search, route planning, weather, or geolocation references in an existing web app.

use_cases.title: Review AMap implementation choices
use_cases.description: Check generated map code against documented lifecycle, plugin, overlay, and security practices.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic map
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a simple AMap JSAPI v2.0 page with a centered 3D map, scale control, toolbar control, and cleanup guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Add markers and info windows
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add several AMap markers with click interactions and info windows. Avoid unsafe dynamic HTML and explain the content strategy.

prompt_templates.title: Integrate search and routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design an AMap workflow that searches for POIs, selects one result, and creates a driving route from a start point.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a production map architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a production AMap JSAPI integration with loader configuration, plugin loading, key protection, service proxying, and resource cleanup.

output_examples.input: Create a basic AMap page for Beijing with zoom controls.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan covering loader setup, required key placeholders, map container sizing, 3D view options, scale and toolbar controls, and component cleanup.

output_examples.input: Add POI search and route planning to an existing map.
output_examples.output: A service integration outline that names the needed AMap plugins, describes the search result flow, and explains how selected locations feed the routing service.

output_examples.input: Review an AMap production setup for security risks.
output_examples.output: A checklist that flags exposed security keys, missing proxy configuration, unsafe HTML in info windows, missing destroy calls, and excessive plugin loading.

best_practices: Use a backend proxy for production security keys and keep browser-exposed keys restricted.
Load only the AMap plugins required by the current page or workflow.
Destroy map instances during component teardown to release WebGL and event resources.
anti_patterns: Do not place production securityJsCode values directly in frontend source code.
Do not build info window content from unsanitized user input.
Do not include broad plugin lists when a page uses only a small subset of AMap services.

faq.question: Does this skill include AMap API keys?
faq.answer: No. It uses placeholders and expects users to provide their own valid AMap JSAPI credentials.

faq.question: Can this skill generate complete map pages?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide an assistant to generate complete page structures, but the result still needs local testing.

faq.question: Does it support JSAPI v2.0?
faq.answer: Yes. The references and examples focus on AMap JSAPI v2.0 and WebGL map behavior.

faq.question: Can it help with geocoding and routing?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes references for geocoder, route planning, POI search, weather, and geolocation services.

faq.question: Is it safe for production use?
faq.answer: The technical references are useful, but the audit flags mandatory telemetry and unsafe example patterns that require review.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
